title: Have You Ever Seen A Whale?

blanks.sentence: Georgie once saw a _____ while walking
Phil observed whales when travelling along the _____ in Patagonia
Whale watching has gained popularity in _____ of the world
Previously, people engaged in whaling to obtain _____
A global ban on commercial whale killing was introduced by the _____
Three countries still continue _____ today
The largest whale species can reach _____ in length
Blue and humpback whales are capable of swimming at speeds of _____
Early hunters used _____ for catching whales
This traditional method was frequently _____
In the _____, an inventor from Norway created a new weapon
The invention of the _____ dramatically changed whale hunting
Faster boats powered by _____ also contributed to increased whaling
By the 20th century, more than _____ whales had been killed
By the 1970s, only _____ remained
Greenpeace launched a campaign called _____
Recordings of whale sounds, known as the _____, became widely listened to
Activists positioned their boats between _____ and whales
Whales continue to face risks from _____ and plastic pollution
The hunting ban has been in effect for _____ so far

multipleChoice.question: What is Phil's attitude when recalling the whale he saw in Patagonia?
options: A. He is impressed by the experience of seeing it in the wild
B. He feels indifferent because it was a common sight
C. He regrets not seeing more whales that day
D. He finds watching whales boring compared to other animals

multipleChoice.question: What contrast does Georgie highlight about whale watching compared to past human activities?
options: A. Whale watching now supports whaling efforts
B. Whale watching is a non-harmful activity unlike whaling
C. Whale watching involves hunting whales for meat
D. Whale watching is illegal in many coastal areas

multipleChoice.question: What can be inferred about the effect of the International Whaling Commission’s ban?
options: A. It completely stopped all whaling activities worldwide
B. It led to increased whale hunting in Japan and Norway
C. It prevented extinction of many whale species
D. It was only effective for a few years after implementation

multipleChoice.question: What does the phrase "turning point" mean in the context of Svend Foyn’s invention?
options: A. The development of non-lethal whale hunting methods
B. When whale watching became more popular than whaling
C. A moment when whale populations began to recover
D. The start of a major change in whaling practices

multipleChoice.question: What reasoning explains why whales "didn't stand a chance" after 1870?
options: A. Whales became slower and easier to catch
B. New technologies made hunting whales much easier
C. Hunters started protecting whale populations
D. Whales started migrating away from coastal areas

multipleChoice.question: What attitude does Joe Roman express towards Greenpeace's role?
options: A. Neutral and merely descriptive about their activities
B. Praising their effective protection of whales
C. Uncertain about their impact on public opinions
D. Critical of their aggressive methods at sea

multipleChoice.question: Which phrase best describes Greenpeace's use of media according to the transcript?
options: A. They struggled to gain media attention for whales
B. They cleverly used media to influence people’s views
C. They relied on government media coverage exclusively
D. They avoided media and focused on direct action

multipleChoice.question: What is the most likely reason that some whale species are still endangered today?
options: A. They continue to be hunted commercially in large numbers
B. Environmental threats like pollution and ocean acidification persist
C. Whale watching causes significant disturbance to their habitats
D. Whales naturally have very short lifespans and low reproduction rates

multipleChoice.question: What is the main reason the moratorium on whaling is important today?
options: A. It temporarily halts hunting to help whale populations
B. It ensures whales will no longer face danger at all
C. It replaces older laws with permanent protections
D. It allows only small-scale, regulated whaling activities

multipleChoice.question: Why is the vaquita whale surprising as the smallest species?
options: A. Because it is often mistaken for the largest whale
B. Because it is much smaller than what “pygmy” suggests
C. Because it lives in deep ocean areas rarely seen
D. Because 'pygmy' usually implies very large size
